text.title: Tool-Tier Approval Gate Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of inc-tool-tier-approval-gate. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team invents `T2a` for partial customer impact; gate semantics ambiguous; gates drift.
New tool added without tier; agent treats it as T0; first use mutates production.
antipattern.fix: Default-tier MUST be T3 (deny without explicit assignment).

antipattern.symptom: Engineer uses break-glass weekly; bypass becomes the path.
antipattern.fix: Bypass count tracked; >2/month triggers security review.
